title: Hieracium neofilicaule
intro: Hieracium neofilicaule is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its medicinal properties and unique appearance. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium neofilicaule include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium neofilicaule include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Hieracium neofilicaule is classified in the family Asteraceae and belongs to the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as hawkweeds. This classification places Hieracium neofilicaule within the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes daisies, sunflowers, and asters. The species is further divided into two subgroups, the Hieracium section and the Pilosella section, with Hieracium neofilicaule being a member of the latter. The Pilosella section is characterized by its unique leaf and stem morphology, which distinguishes it from other members of the Hieracium genus.
